msContents.summary: Maḥzor for the entire year according to the Western Ashkenazi rite

msItem.title: מחזור מנהג אשכנז המערבי לכל השנה
Maḥzor minhag Ashkenaz ha-maʿaravi le-khol ha-shanah
msItem.note: The second of three volumes; the other two volumes are MS Michael 571 (Neubauer 1097; vol. 1) and MS Michael 573 (Neubauer 1099; vol. 3).
Used by Daniel Goldschmidt in his edition of the Sukkot services (Jerusalem, 1981).

supportDesc.condition: The margins of ff. 116-120 had been repaired with parchment strips taken from an Old French manuscript from the late-13th or early-14th century. These strips were later removed from the folios, leaving some barely-legible imprints of the Old French text in mirror image.

handDesc.handNote: In the short colophon on f. 81r, the scribe's name is given as Izaḳ איזק. On ff. 86r, 91v, 111v., the name יצחק (Isaac) has been picked out by the scribal hand.

decoDesc.p: Penwork decorations in black and red, most of which are found around initial words; these include arched doorways (ff. 1r, 6v, 27v, 108v, 115v), roundels (ff. 49r, 105r, 116r), rectangular panels (ff. 70r, 72r) and drawings of animals (ff. 26r, 27r, 37v, 81r). Some of the catchwords (ff. 24v, 32v, 40v, 112v, 120v) have been decorated with animal or foliate motifs, while others only have very simple geometric decoration formed from dotted lines.

bindingDesc.p: Golden-brown fine-grained leather over paper boards; the current shelfmark has been gold-tooled and painted (in black and white) on the spine.

note.p: The dating is based on the calendar on f. 95r, which begins mid-cycle from the year 5049 (1288/9).

history.provenance: In the late-sixteenth century, all three volumes of this Maḥzor were owned by members of the Ḳoser קוצר family, including Meshullam b. Mordecai Ḳoser משולם בן מרדכי קוצר (MS Michael 571, f. 134v) and his brother Menaḥem מנחם who recorded the birth of his son in 1582 (MS Michael 573, f. 45v). This is possibly the same as the Ḳoser קוצר family in Riva del Garda, where a tombstone belonging to one Meshullam Ḳoser משולם קוצר who died in 1541 has survived, while a Mordecai Ḳoser מרדכי קוצר is attested in 1564 (see Kaufmann 1895, 1896, 1897). These may have been the grandfather and father of the owners who left inscriptions in the manuscript.
history.acquisition: It belonged to the library of Heimann Joseph Michael (1792-1846), which was purchased en bloc (altogether 862 volumes) by the Bodleian Library in 1848 through Messrs. Asher and Co. for £1030.

recordHist.source: Manuscript description based on Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, vol. I, by Adolf Neubauer, Oxford 1886, No. 1098, Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I, No. 1098, and on the data of the Institute of Microfilmed Hebrew Manuscripts, National Library of Israel with additional enhancements by the cataloguer.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card (for admissions procedures contact Bodleian Admissions). Contact specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript.

listBibl.bibl: Neubauer, Adolf, Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library (Oxford, 1886), no. 1098.
Kaufmann, David, 'L'inscription à Riva', Revue des études juives 31:62 (1895): 292-293.
Kaufmann, David, 'La pierre tumulaire de Meschoullam Kocer à Riva', Revue des études juives 33:66 (1896): 311-314.
Kaufmann, David, 'La famille קוצר ou Cousseri à Riva', Revue des études juives 35:70 (1897): 302-304.

Beit-Arié, Malachi and R.A. May (eds), Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I (A. Neubauer’s Catalogue) (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1994), no. 1098.
